ip_outcome_note: Card Category: FFL (Final Fantasy Legends/Dimensions) and canonical visual match — young dancer with flower-tiara and gold coin-chain attire matches the Dimensions Matoya: fourth in a lineage of women bearing that name, a dancer over 100 years old who leads the Desert Moon rebels. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Matoya EX (8-063R) is a Wind-element Backup (Job: Dancer, Category: FFL, Cost: 4) from Opus VIII. EX BURST: when Matoya enters the field, choose 1 Monster opponent controls and Break it. Opus VIII featured exclusive Kira Oguro illustrations for FFL characters. [ArcadeGameCards listing; Square Enix Opus VIII release page]

This Matoya is from Final Fantasy Dimensions (FFL in Japan), not the FF1 witch. She is the fourth woman in her family bearing the name — a dancer appearing young but over 100 years old, who leads rebel faction Desert Moon against the empire of Avalon. Her flower-tiara and Crystal Eye are canonical signature visual identifiers. [Final Fantasy Wiki: Matoya (Dimensions)]

Opus VIII featured exclusive illustrations by Kira Oguro for Final Fantasy Legends characters, making Matoya EX part of a deliberate artist spotlight on the FFL/Dimensions roster. [Square Enix Opus VIII release page]

A 4-cost Wind Backup with on-entry Monster removal fills a niche: Wind decks rarely access direct destruction, making Matoya EX's trigger meaningful utility in the Backup slot. [WebSearch snippets: TCG Republic, ArcadeGameCards]
